brand.name: Boyum Law
brand.vertical: Local & service / B2B professional (legal — estate planning & probate)

summary: Boyum Law is a solo, Nebraska-licensed law firm in the Omaha metro (attorney Brad Boyum) handling estate planning — wills, living trusts, special needs and pet trusts, farm succession, and asset protection — plus probate administration, elder law, powers of attorney, and Medicaid planning. It serves Omaha-area families aged roughly 45–70 with accumulated assets who haven't formalized a plan, and adult children or spouses navigating probate after a death. Engagements are one-off, project-based legal fees, and the free initial consultation is the primary way clients are won.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Turn a new inquiry or consultation request into a confident, prepared lead who shows up to the free consultation knowing exactly what to expect.
campaigns.trigger: Contact form submitted / free-consultation requested / email list join.
campaigns.segment: New contacts; branch by stated need (pre-planning family vs. post-loss probate vs. farm/business owner) where the intake form captures it.
campaigns.rationale: Every backbone campaign is missing today, so the playbook's #1 priority applies cleanly: this is the highest-engagement, fastest-to-value send and it sets the trust that a sensitive, family-first legal purchase depends on. For Boyum, the inquiry-to-consultation moment is where intent is hottest and where most leads currently fall silent.

emails.purpose: Instant warm welcome the moment someone reaches out: reassure them, set expectations for the free consultation, and deliver one quick win (clarity on what the meeting covers and that there is zero obligation).
emails.subject: We got your message — here's what your free consultation covers
emails.preview: No legal jargon, no obligation. Just a clear plan for protecting your family.
keyPoints: Reassure: reaching out is the hard part, and there's no cost and no obligation to retain after the consultation
Set expectations for the 30–60 min meeting with Brad — he reviews your specific situation and shows you exactly which documents (will, trust, POA) you actually need and why
Tell them what to bring (rough list of assets, who they'd name) so the meeting is productive
Plain-English, family-first tone; a real reply-to (Brad/firm) and the firm name + Omaha address for CAN-SPAM and NE Bar compliance
Disclaimer: this email does not create an attorney-client relationship

campaigns.name: Free-Consultation Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert inquiries who haven't yet booked — and fence-sitters weighing DIY tools — into a booked, retained free consultation by answering the #1 objection head-on.
campaigns.trigger: Inquiry made but consultation not yet booked, or lead engaged with content but stalled before booking.
campaigns.segment: Unbooked / stalled leads; branch by whether they cited cost concern vs. 'not urgent yet' procrastination.
campaigns.rationale: The free consultation is Boyum's entire close mechanism, so the core revenue play (playbook #2) is to get unbooked leads into that meeting. The single biggest blocker is 'do I really need a lawyer, or is LegalZoom enough?' — answering that with candor is what moves an Omaha family from curious to committed.

emails.purpose: The hero send: meet the LegalZoom/DIY objection honestly, show the real cost of getting it wrong in Nebraska, and make booking the no-risk next step.
emails.subject: LegalZoom or a lawyer? An honest answer for Nebraska families
emails.preview: When a $99 template is fine — and when it quietly costs your heirs far more.
keyPoints: Lead with candor: name when a simple online will genuinely is enough — this honesty builds the trust the brand is known for
Then the real risk: a generic template that isn't properly executed or funded under Nebraska law can send the house through probate anyway, costing heirs time and money
Reframe the consultation as the low-risk way to find out which camp you're in — free, no obligation, 30–60 minutes with Brad
Gently counter procrastination: planning is easiest before a health event forces the issue
No outcome guarantees (NE Bar rules); firm name/address + not-legal-advice disclaimer

campaigns.name: Booking Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize consultation show-up rate and recover the appointments that slip, protecting the firm's primary revenue event.
campaigns.trigger: Consultation booked; upcoming appointment; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled free consultation with Brad.
campaigns.rationale: Because the free consultation IS the close, a missed appointment is lost revenue — and reminders cut no-shows ~35% on average. The playbook ranks appointment-driven reminders #3 for exactly this kind of brand, and Boyum has no reminder automation today.

emails.purpose: Hero send: the 24-hour-before reminder that locks in attendance — confirms time, lowers anxiety about a sensitive topic, gives prep + easy reschedule so a conflict becomes a rebook instead of a no-show.
emails.subject: See you tomorrow, {{first_name}} — your consultation with Brad
emails.preview: A few things to have handy so we make the most of your time.
emails.cta: Add to calendar / need to reschedule?
keyPoints: Confirm date, time, and location (1227 S 119th St, Omaha) or phone-call details
Quick prep: jot down your assets and who you'd want to make decisions — no homework stress, just helps Brad tailor the review
Reassure: this is a relaxed, judgment-free conversation, not a sales pitch; no obligation to retain
One-click reschedule link to convert conflicts into rebooks rather than no-shows
Firm name/address and not-legal-advice disclaimer for compliance

campaigns.name: Estate-Planning Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move cold contacts and content consumers — who downloaded a guide or listened to the audio interviews but aren't ready to book — toward consultation-readiness over a longer cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Gated guide downloaded or audio-interview signup (e.g., 'The Omaha Family's Estate Planning Starter Guide').
campaigns.segment: Leads below the consultation-ready threshold; branch by topic (basic estate planning vs. probate help vs. farm/business succession).
campaigns.rationale: Boyum already has low-friction content (resources page, expert audio interviews) but no sequence to convert those passive consumers. The playbook puts nurture at #4 once a magnet feeds the funnel; for a long-procrastination purchase like estate planning, patient education is what eventually triggers the booking.

emails.purpose: Hero send: deliver the requested guide instantly and frame the real problem behind it — most Nebraska families assume a will covers them, and that gap is what costs heirs.
emails.subject: Your estate planning guide is inside, {{first_name}}
emails.preview: The one assumption that quietly sends a Nebraska home through probate.
keyPoints: Deliver the asset immediately with a clear download/link — fulfill the promise first
Frame the core problem: a will alone often still means probate in Nebraska; many families don't realize the gap until it's too late
Set the cadence: over the next few weeks, short plain-English emails on wills vs. trusts, protecting the farm/business, and what probate really involves
Soft, no-pressure invite to the free consultation whenever they're ready — no urgency manufactured
Firm name/address, unsubscribe, and not-legal-advice disclaimer for CAN-SPAM / NE Bar compliance

campaigns.name: Win-Back & Plan-Review Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant past clients and cold leads, and surface the families whose lives have changed and whose estate plan now needs updating — protecting list deliverability while recovering revenue.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold (60–90 days no engagement) for cold leads; 2–3 years since last engagement for past clients.
campaigns.segment: Dormant subscribers/leads (lean on fresh value); separately, past planning clients (lean on life-change plan review).
campaigns.rationale: Win-back is the playbook's #5 — quick to ship and it protects deliverability — and for Boyum it doubles as the answer to the firm's biggest churn risk: one-and-done clients who never update documents and become someone else's probate problem. Reactivating them is both retention and new revenue.

emails.purpose: Hero send: the gentle 'has your life changed?' nudge that reminds past clients an estate plan isn't set-and-forget — and re-opens the door for a review without pressure.
emails.subject: Has your life changed since we last talked, {{first_name}}?
emails.preview: A new grandchild, a home, a move — small changes your plan may need to catch up on.
keyPoints: Warm, no-guilt reconnect: it's been a while, and that's normal — but plans should keep pace with life
List the common triggers that make a plan outdated: marriage/divorce, a new child or grandchild, a major asset or home purchase, a move, or a death in the family
Make the next step low-friction: a short review to confirm everything still reflects their wishes — no obligation
For cold leads (separate branch): lead with fresh value/content rather than a review ask, and include a clean opt-out to sunset non-responders and protect deliverability
Firm name/address, unsubscribe, and not-legal-advice disclaimer for compliance
